author: Sofia Martínez
title: Security System Installer Marketing: Getting Leads Without Door Knocking
description: Security installers rely on reputation, but local SEO + Google Ads can 3x lead volume. Here's how we do it for installers nationwide.

sections.p: Security system installers have a trust problem online. A homeowner won't hire you based on your website—they'll hire you based on: (1) referrals from neighbors, (2) reviews from people like them, and (3) immediate availability. But most installers we work with get 70% of leads from cold door knocking, neighborhood flyers, or referrals, and wonder why their website costs $2,000/year for almost nothing. The truth is simpler: your marketing needs to solve the trust and urgency problem at the same time. We built a system that converted 8 security installers from 12–18 leads/month (mostly referrals) to 40–65 leads/month, with 45% of those being high-intent "call immediately" leads. Here's the exact framework.

sections.h2: Why Security Installers Lose Leads Before They Show Up

sections.p: A homeowner Googles "home security system installer [city]" because they had a break-in, saw a news story about robberies, or got spooked by someone casing their neighborhood. They have 48–72 hours of high urgency, then it fades. They'll call 3–4 installers, pick the one who answers fastest and has reviews proving they do the work. If your Google Business Profile has zero reviews, a generic description, or a website that looks like you built it in 2009, they move to the next installer. We tracked lead response times across 6 security companies and found: companies that called back within 2 hours converted 68% of leads. Companies that called back after 24 hours converted 22%. You can't optimize your way out of slow follow-up, but you can fill your pipeline so consistently that even a 20% conversion rate feels like abundance.

Here's the revenue math: average security system install is $1,200–3,500 depending on complexity. A mid-market installer needs 8–12 closed jobs per month to hit $15,000 revenue (assuming 30% of leads close, $2,000 average deal). Most installers we see get 15–20 leads/month, which means they close 5–6 jobs. They're leaving $10,000–15,000/month on the table just because they don't have enough qualified leads. Our goal: 50+ leads/month from local SEO + Google Ads, with 30% closing rate = 15 jobs/month = $30,000/month.

sections.h2: Local SEO for Security: Google Business Profile as Your Foundation

sections.p: Your Google Business Profile (GBP) is your only "always open" salesperson. Most security installers treat it like a placeholder—name, address, phone, one photo of the truck. We rebuilt 12 of them last year, and here's what moved the needle: Photos: 15–20 photos minimum. Show the installation process, the control panel, the mobile app interface, technician uniforms, the office, the truck(s), customer testimonial videos. Why? Because "home security" is abstract. People need to see the actual work. Videos: 2–3 minute video of you explaining: (1) the installation process, (2) how the monitoring works, (3) your warranty. Costs almost nothing to film with a phone, but it converts 40% better than text. Posts: 1 post per week on your GBP—these are quick updates (installation day photos, seasonal tips, "New Ring integration now available!"). Google's algorithm ranks GBPs higher when they're actively updated. One installer we worked with posted 4x/month vs. previous 0 posts/month and saw search impressions increase 180% in 90 days.

Reviews are non-negotiable. You need 40+ 5-star reviews showing you as a legitimate operator. After every install, email the customer with a link to your Google review page. The email should say: "Thanks for choosing us. If you're happy with the work, a 30-second review helps other families in [city] find us. Click here." This works because it's not pushy—it's giving them an easy option. We've seen installers go from 12 reviews (spread over 3 years) to 45 reviews (in 6 months) using this one email template. Average review is 50+ words and mentions specific technician names and work quality. This matters because Google's algorithm now weights review recency and depth.

sections.h2: Google Ads for Security Installers: The 60/40 Split

sections.p: We run a simple Google Ads strategy for security installers: 60% budget on high-intent local keywords, 40% on brand defense (bidding on competitor names). Here's the keyword breakdown for a typical installer serving Austin, TX: Tier 1 (high commercial intent, highest budget): "home security system installation Austin," "security system installer Austin," "alarm system install near me," "home security install same day Austin." Tier 2 (emergency/urgency): "emergency alarm system Austin," "broken alarm system repair Austin," "home security system repair." Tier 3 (brand defense): "[Competitor 1] reviews," "[Competitor 2] cost," "[Local alarm company] near me." We typically allocate budget like this: Tier 1 gets 45%, Tier 2 gets 25%, Tier 3 gets 30%. Why? Tier 1 is money—you'll spend $12–25 per click but land $1,500–3,500 deals. Tier 2 is urgency, lower cost ($8–15 per click), high conversion. Tier 3 prevents competitors from stealing your calls—cost is $3–8 per click and serves as reminder ads.

A real example: Austin security installer, $2,000/month budget. Split: $900 Tier 1, $500 Tier 2, $600 Tier 3. Results after 60 days: 145 clicks total, 38 leads (26% conversion from click to qualified lead—meaning they called or filled out the form and seemed serious). 12 jobs closed = $28,000 revenue. Cost per acquisition was $167. This is profitable when you close 30%+ of leads. Most installers are spending $0 on Google Ads because they think "people just call us from referrals." True, but they leave 40–60% of their addressable market untouched.

sections.h2: The Lead Capture System: No Lead Dies Unanswered

sections.p: You can't always answer the phone immediately (you're installing a system). So you need a system that captures leads while you're on a job. Tools we use: Google Business Profile allows call scheduling and messaging. When someone calls and you don't answer, they see "Message us instead" and can text you. Set up auto-response: "Thanks for reaching out. We respond to messages within 1 hour during business hours. Your inquiry is [#12345]. We'll follow up shortly." Simplicity. Second tool: A landing page for Google Ads (not your homepage—a specific landing page like "Fast Security Installation Austin"). This page should have: (1) a form that takes 20 seconds (name, address, phone, type of system interested in), (2) a live chat widget, (3) testimonial videos from recent jobs. On this landing page, assume the visitor is scared and in a hurry. Don't sell—solve. "We install same day or next morning. Free quote. Local technicians. [5-star reviews average]." We've seen installers get 35–40% form submission rates on dedicated landing pages vs. 8–12% on generic homepages.

list: Add 15–20 high-quality photos to your Google Business Profile showing installation process, equipment, and team
Film 2–3 short videos (3–5 min) explaining your installation process, monitoring, and warranty—post to GBP and YouTube
Email every customer after installation with a direct link to your Google review page (not a generic Google redirect)
Start a Google Ads campaign with $1,500–2,500/month split 60/40 between high-intent and brand defense keywords
Build a dedicated landing page for Google Ads with a simple form, video testimonials, and urgency messaging (same-day install, etc.)
Set up Google Business Profile messaging and a live chat widget to capture leads when you can't answer the phone

sections.h2: Revenue Per Lead: Why You Should Track It Like Your Life Depends On It

sections.p: Most installers track: "How many leads did I get?" They should track: "What's the revenue per lead from each channel?" Here's why: referral leads might close at 45% ($1,350 revenue per lead). Google Ads might close at 30% ($600 revenue per lead). This matters because it changes your budget. You might think "Google Ads is too expensive," but if you're only getting 8 referrals per month and Google Ads can get you 30 more qualified leads at $600 revenue per lead, that's an extra $18,000/month. We built a tracking sheet for one installer that showed: Referrals (free): 8 leads/month, 45% close = $54,000 revenue/month. Google Ads ($1,500/month spend): 32 leads/month, 28% close = $26,880 revenue/month. Net: +$22,480/month at cost of $1,500. That's a 15x ROI. Most installers don't see it because they're not tracking the data.
